message:
feat(test-logging): add CI correlation ID support to structured test log framework

Adds an optional `ci_correlation_id` field to the structured test
logging framework (pi.test.log.v2 schema) that is automatically
populated from the `CI_CORRELATION_ID` environment variable. This
enables correlating test log records across parallel CI jobs,
distributed test runs, and multi-agent build pipelines.

Changes to TestLogger:
- New `ci_correlation_id: Option<String>` field, populated from env
  var in both `new()` and `with_min_level()` constructors
- New `ci_correlation_id()` accessor method
- Propagated through `build_log_record()` into every emitted JSONL
  record via `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`

Changes to TestLogJsonRecord:
- New optional `ci_correlation_id` field in the serialized structure

Changes to JSONL validation:
- `validate_jsonl_line()` now validates that `ci_correlation_id`, if
  present, is a string (rejects numeric/bool/null values)

Also adds deprecation notice to `TEST_LOG_SCHEMA_V1` constant
referencing DISC-021 / bd-38m8w for the v1→v2 migration rationale.

Test coverage:
- `ci_correlation_id_absent_when_env_unset`: verifies field is omitted
  from JSON when env var is not set
- `ci_correlation_id_in_jsonl_passes_validation`: v2 record with
  string ci_correlation_id validates successfully
- `ci_correlation_id_without_value_passes_validation`: v2 record
  without the field also validates (backward compat)
- `v2_schema_rejects_non_string_ci_correlation_id`: ensures type
  validation catches non-string values

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
